# .github

- reviewdog.yaml builds sisakulint itself on every PR and dogfoods it over all workflows via SARIF. Workflow edits that violate sisakulint's own rules such as commit SHA pinning of actions and explicit permissions get flagged automatically on the PR.
- CI.yaml only shows the gofmt diff; it runs no tests or linters and never fails. Local go test ./... is the only behavioral guarantee.
- Releases fire from release.yml on a v*.*.* tag push, running goreleaser and a Homebrew tap update that needs the TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN PAT. The tap target still disagrees: release.yml pushes to ultra-supara/... while .goreleaser.yml targets sisaku-security/....
- The effective config is .github/sisakulint.yaml. The action.yaml generated by -init is never read and is gitignored.
